what_it_does: Defines the ConceptV design system README, including product entities, source assets, content voice, visual foundations, shape rules, iconography, and UI kit locations.
observations: Frames ConceptV as an Australian innovation-technology company built around Universal Mechanics.
Lists four connected entities: User Portal, Property Wizard, Virtual Hubs, and Vi, each with an assigned shape.
Documents source materials used for the system, including logos, screenshots, icon libraries, illustrations, and an image-only pitch deck.
States that the public website was offline during exploration and that calibration used mockups and provided assets.
Defines content voice, vocabulary, numeric labels, visual tokens, spacing, radii, borders, shadows, motion, and fixed layout elements.
Describes three icon families: emoji sidebar icons, the CV vector icon library, and gold-circle entity badges.
Lists platform, virtual-hub, and Vi UI kit folders and their covered surfaces.
standouts: The entity-to-shape mapping is treated as a recurring brand rule.
The README combines brand voice, visual tokens, interface behavior, and asset inventory in one file.
The iconography section separates navigation emoji, vector line icons, and entity-badge treatments by use case.
conventions: Uses sentence-case headings and descriptive prose with structured tables and bullets.
Treats colors as named design tokens with hex values and usage guidance.
Uses explicit do-and-do-not style guidance for copy, colors, imagery, motion, transparency, and icons.
Preserves file path references for assets and kits.
Includes implementation-oriented snippets for using the CvIcon React component.
concerns: The title says ConceptV Design System while the content also includes product strategy and brand narrative beyond README-level setup.
Font substitution is flagged as unresolved because the real product font is unknown.
The file says colors were sampled pixel-accurate from assets, but the evidence trail is only described, not embedded.
notes: Brief someone on this as the ConceptV design-system orientation document for brand, UI, copy, shapes, icons, and kit locations.
questions: What exact production font should replace the Sora and DM Sans substitutions?
Are the observed mockup URL patterns and entity terms final product language?
Should the Vi UI kit path listed in the table also appear in the root-folder manifest?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it presents a design system and supplies supporting rules, assets, vocabulary, and UI kit references.
apparent_themes: Warm ivory and gold visual identity
intention_signals: It seems intended to guide designers and implementers toward consistent ConceptV-branded outputs across decks, dashboards, virtual hubs, diagrams, and AI workspace surfaces.
novelty: The file's notable distinction is the combination of platform entities with fixed geometric shapes, emoji-based sidebar navigation, and gold-ring line-icon entity badges.
contribution: It contributes structured ConceptV design rules, entity vocabulary, shape semantics, color and typography tokens, icon usage patterns, UI kit inventory, and content guidance that a fused system would keep as reference material.
summary_for_embedding: This is a ConceptV design-system README describing product entities, brand voice, visual tokens, layout rules, shape semantics, icon families, source assets, and UI kit folders.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to orient future ConceptV design and implementation work around consistent brand, interface, content, and asset conventions.
